input.background: Refer to the first-order model for auction price $(y)$ considered in Example 12.1.
input.question: Explain why $	ext{hat}{ eta}_0$ does not have a meaningful interpretation in this example.

output.answer: The value $	ext{hat}{ eta}_0=-1,339$ does not have a meaningful interpretation in this example. To see this, note that $	ext{hat}{y}=	ext{hat}{ eta}_0$ when $x_1=x_2=0$. Thus, $	ext{hat}{ eta}_0=-1,339$ represents the estimated mean auction price when the values of all the independent variables are set equal to 0. Because an antique clock with these characteristics - an age of 0 years and 0 bidders on the clock-is not practical, the value of $	ext{hat}{ eta}_0$ has no meaningful interpretation.

meta_info.caption: Interpreting the $\beta$ Estimates: Clock Auction Price Model
